sevenDayForecast.headline: Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol has intensified to a Category 4 cyclone.
points: Tropical Cyclone Advices and Forecast Track Maps are being issued for Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol and should be referred to for more detailed information.
Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol is expected to remain Category 4 tonight, before beginning to weaken quickly from Thursday afternoon.
Errol is currently slow moving, but is expected to start moving to the east southeast later tonight or during Thursday morning, taking it towards the west Kimberley coast.

trackForecast.headline: Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol has intensified to a Category 4 cyclone and may be starting to move to the southwest before its expected recurve towards the west Kimberley coast on Thursday.
trackForecast.discussion:
Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol (29U) has intensified to a Category 4 cyclone with a clear eye on satellite imagery, this means our confidence in the location is high. Errol may continue to intensify a little further overnight. The last few hours motions suggest it may have started to move to the southwest before it begins to recurves towards the southeast during Thursday.  

Conditions are expected to become unfavourable during Thursday with Increased wind shear and dry air likely to begin weakening Errol. Weakening is likely to be rapid due to the small size of Errol and the system is forecast to drop to a Category 1 as it nears the west Kimberley coast during Friday.  

The extension of the 120 hour confidence cone towards offshore Pilbara waters is representing scenarios of a remnant weak shallow tropical low moving back towards the west over the weekend. A tropical cyclone is very unlikely to exist in this area.
trackForecast.discussionUpperBound: The upper bound scenario is based on a period of continued intensification over the next 12 hours. As Errol is a small system, it is prone to rapid fluctuations in intensity. The upper bound from later Thursday reflects a delay in the weakening associated with the negative impacts of the upper trough, allowing the system to remain stronger than forecast as it moves nearer the Kimberley coast.
